title: ClientInner field-drop order must survive future edits
description: The ClientInner struct relies on Rust's declaration-order drop guarantee: client_ctx must be cleaned up (by the explicit Drop impl) before comm_cfg is dropped, and comm_cfg must outlive transport. This is explained in a block comment above the struct, but the struct fields themselves carry no indication of the constraint. A contributor who adds a field or reorders fields for aesthetic reasons silently breaks the invariant with no compile error. Each field should carry an inline comment stating its drop-order role, e.g. '// dropped 3rd — must outlive comm_cfg' on transport, so the ordering contract is visible exactly where future edits happen.
